meaning: adj.保持原味的,活泼的,适于赛跑的,<美>猥亵的
examples: He listened to David Bright's racy stories about life in the navy.
The publications are similar to "A&F Quarterly", a racy magalog also produced by Weber.
Her shoes, which nobody got photos of, were a bit more racy.
These racy poems shed light on the lives of regular people in medieval times.
Some racy tires and aftermarket suspension tuning would transform the handling of this car.
